freshness_caveat: Coal data stale (164 days old, as of 2025-12-26) and Clean Dark stale (159 days old, as of 2025-12-31). Fuel-switch spread dynamics and thermal merit-order position not current; treat derived spreads with caution until refresh.
risk_flags: Clean Spark 94th percentile (43.16 EUR/MWh) with +110 EUR/MWh daily move suggests volatility spike; grid capacity stress from AI demand surge could widen intraday spreads.
Renewables at 93rd percentile (70.34% of load) but shipping disruption threatens LNG import cost — arbitrage pressure on TTF if cargoes face 20-30% premium; regional power spikes possible.
EU-China sanctions escalation (solar, EV battery, rare-earth imports) risks renewable capacity delays and inadvertent fossil fuel margin support if green transition stutters.
Coal data stale (164 days old); Clean Dark stale (159 days old) — spread dynamics opaque; recent DE Power +263% daily move (152.12 EUR/MWh, 80th percentile) not yet reconciled to fundamentals.

base.summary: Renewables remain elevated; Clean Spark stays 90th+ percentile. AI demand announcements prop power intraday spreads; LNG spot cargoes trade 10-15% premium on shipping friction.

downside.summary: US-EU tariff truce holds; shipping disruption eases via insurance/corridor solutions. Renewables auction fills accelerate; oversupply of wind/solar in grid depresses DA spreads. Coal recovers from multi-year lows.

upside.summary: EU-China sanctions escalate; solar/EV battery imports disrupted, renewable capex delayed. LNG cargoes face 25-30% shipping premium; arbitrage drives TTF spike. Peak shaving mandate tightens grid capacity.

themes: Structural EU power demand surge from AI and industry
Renewables at historical highs; green capex fiscal exemption accelerates build
Geopolitical friction (China sanctions, shipping disruption) threatens LNG reliability and cost
Coal historically depressed (7th percentile); fuel-switch regime persists
top_drivers: Renewables at 93rd percentile (70.34% of load) suppressing marginal-cost power pricing; Clean Spark 94th percentile reflects structural AI/industrial demand surge competing for electrons.
Shipping disruption and LNG import cost inflation via geopolitical friction (China sanctions, supply-chain fragmentation) — TTF arbitrage upside if cargoes face sustained premium.
Coal remains depressed (7th percentile, 96 USD/t) but fuel-switch regime stable; fiscal exemption for green capex accelerates renewables build, extending thermal displacement.
top_takeaway: Clean Spark at 94th percentile on structural EU power demand surge from AI/industry; renewables at 93rd percentile offset by shipping-driven LNG risk and China sanctions friction.

watchlist.metric: EU AI datacenter announcements & grid stress testing
watchlist.why: Politico reporting structural electricity demand surge from AI; peak-shaving mandates could flatten load curves and raise Cal+1 volatility.

watchlist.metric: LNG shipping cost indices & spot cargo premiums to NW Europe
watchlist.why: Permanent shipping disruption threatens reliable LNG import; +20-30% shipping premium would elevate TTF via arbitrage and tighten regional power margins.

watchlist.metric: EU-China sanctions implementation pace & solar/EV supply retaliation
watchlist.why: Renewable capex delays indirectly support fossil fuel economics; also affects long-dated green capacity pricing.

watchlist.metric: Q3 2026 renewables auction calendar & fiscal rule exemption fund deployment
watchlist.why: Green capex acceleration via fiscal exemption will shape medium-term power supply and Cal+2+ curve structure.
Trade escalation on green tech imports could delay renewable build and constrain EU industrial competitiveness.
